title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, a delicious Korean seafood pancake, with our easy step-by-step recipe. Perfect for a savory snack or appetizer!
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Perfect Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A golden-brown Haemul Pajeon (Korean seafood pancake) with crispy edges, filled with various seafood and scallions, served on a plate with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Korea with this homemade Haemul Pajeon, a savory and satisfying seafood pancake perfect for any occasion!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved savory Korean pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges and tender interior packed with an abundance of fresh seafood and vibrant green scallions. This classic dish offers a delightful textural contrast and a burst of oceanic flavors, making it a perfect appetizer or light meal. Enjoy this authentic taste of Korea, best served hot with a tangy dipping sauce.

ingredients.notes: cleaned, sliced into rings or bite-sized pieces
for frying, plus more as needed

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, and cold water until smooth. Add the lightly beaten eggs and mix until just combined. Do not overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable. Set the batter aside to rest for 10-15 minutes while you prepare other ingredients.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and prepare your seafood: cut shrimp and squid into bite-sized pieces. If using mussels or clams, ensure they are clean and shelled. Cut the green onions into 3-inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion. For the dipping sauce, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, water, sesame oil, minced garlic, sesame seeds, and a pinch of sugar in a small bowl.

instructions.title: Assemble the Pancake Mixture
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood, green onions, and sliced yellow onion directly into the batter bowl. Gently fold the ingredients into the batter until everything is evenly coated. Be careful not to mash the ingredients. The batter should be just enough to bind everything together.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan and Add Batter
instructions.text: Heat a large, non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add about 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the bottom evenly. Once the oil is shimmering hot, pour about 1/4 of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it out to form a thin, even pancake, about 8-10 inches in diameter. You may need to gently arrange the green onions and seafood with chopsticks or a spatula.

instructions.title: Cook Until Golden and Crispy
instructions.text: Cook the pancake for 5-7 minutes on one side, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the top begins to set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake to ensure crispiness. Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. If desired, you can crack an additional egg directly onto the cooked side before flipping for extra richness.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking and Serve
instructions.text: Cook the second side for another 5-7 minutes, or until deeply golden brown and cooked through. Press down gently with the spatula to ensure even cooking and crispness. Remove the cooked Haemul Pajeon from the pan and place it on a cutting board. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil as needed for each pancake. Slice the pancake into wedges and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For extra crispiness, use a combination of all-purpose flour and rice flour. A very hot pan and sufficient oil are crucial for achieving that desired golden-brown crust.

Feel free to customize the seafood mix based on your preference or what's available fresh. Mussels, clams, oysters, or even firm white fish can be excellent additions. Ensure all seafood is fresh and properly cleaned.

Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal texture. For the dipping sauce, adjust the spice level by adding a pinch of Korean chili flakes (gochugaru) or finely chopped fresh chilies.
article: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake is a beloved Korean Seafood dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
